title: Gnocchi with Sage Butter Sauce

overview.description: Soft potato gnocchi tossed in nutty brown butter with fresh sage and finished with Parmesan. A beautifully simple Italian dish that proves a handful of good ingredients can make an exceptional meal.

method: Bring a large pan of well-salted water to the boil.
Cook the gnocchi according to the packet instructions until they float to the surface, then drain well.
Meanwhile, melt the butter in a large frying pan over a medium heat.
Continue cooking until the butter turns golden brown and smells nutty, about 3–5 minutes.
Add the sage leaves and cook for about 30 seconds until crisp.
Add the drained gnocchi and toss gently to coat in the browned butter.
Remove from the heat and stir through most of the Parmesan.
Season with freshly ground black pepper and a little salt if needed.
Serve immediately with the remaining Parmesan sprinkled over the top.

learning.did-you-know: Brown butter, known in French as beurre noisette, develops a rich nutty flavour as the milk solids gently caramelise.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to brown butter without burning it, why sage pairs so well with butter, and how simple dishes rely on careful technique rather than lots of ingredients.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the simplest meals in the planner and an excellent reminder that outstanding food doesn't always need long ingredient lists or complex techniques.
chapman-tips: Watch the butter carefully once it starts foaming—it can go from browned to burnt in seconds.
Fresh sage makes a noticeable difference.
Buy good quality gnocchi rather than making it from scratch unless you want the challenge.
notes.batch-cooking: Not suitable for freezing once cooked.
notes.leftovers: Best eaten immediately, although leftovers can be lightly fried until crisp the following day.
